title: Control proximity
summary: Keep controls inside or directly adjacent to the viewer, panel, or artifact they change so users can immediately tell what surface they govern.
decision: Whether a local control is placed inside or directly adjacent to the surface region it changes, rather than in a separate zone that obscures ownership.

intent.purpose: Prevent generated interfaces from separating local controls from the viewer, panel, or artifact they govern until the user can no longer easily tell which surface will change.
intent.failure_mode: Generated UI places a local view control in a distant header, metadata band, or unrelated action cluster, so the user has to infer which pane or artifact it affects.
intent.good_judgment: Place local view controls in the local viewer toolbar or directly beside the governed surface. Keep global navigation and cross-surface links outside that zone, visually secondary, and clearly distinct from the local control cluster.
acceptable_variation: compress local controls into an overflow or segmented control on narrow screens when the control still stays attached to the governed surface
place a local control immediately above or beside the affected viewer when layout density requires a compact toolbar
keep global navigation or raw-file links elsewhere when they are visually secondary and do not read as part of the local view cluster
follow approved design-system toolbar or inspector patterns when surface ownership remains obvious
non_negotiables: no local view toggle parked in a separate header or details band while it affects a lower viewer or sibling pane
no mixed local-view controls and global navigation actions presented as one undifferentiated cluster
no placement that forces the user to guess which surface, pane, or artifact a control changes
no cross-surface action given equal visual ownership to the local viewer controls it does not govern

detection.decision_question: Does each control sit inside or directly adjacent to the surface it changes, or is it positioned in a separate zone that makes control ownership ambiguous?
signals: a control floats in a header or details band while affecting a lower viewer
local viewer controls are mixed with global navigation or cross-surface links in one cluster
a button appears to belong to the wrong pane or region
the controlled surface has no obvious nearby toolbar or local action zone
the user must visually scan across zones to infer what the control governs

thresholds.pass: affected-surface ownership is obvious because local controls live inside or directly adjacent to the governed region
thresholds.warn: control placement creates ambiguity but can be fixed with relayout without changing the information architecture
thresholds.fail: a user can plausibly misattribute which surface the control changes

low.description: Relayout the local control so it sits with the surface it governs.

medium.description: Relayout the control cluster and route the result through design review for ownership clarity.

high.description: Block the output and require design review because users can no longer reliably tell what surface a control changes.
